title: lomariopsis novae-caledoniae
intro: Lomariopsis novae-caledoniae, also known as the New Caledonia Lomariopsis, is a species of fern in the family Lomariopsidaceae. This plant is native to New Caledonia and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The leaves of Lomariopsis novae-caledoniae contain flavonoids and other compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the leaves are often made into a tea or infused in hot water to take advantage of its health benefits, including the relief of coughs and colds, as well as its ability to act as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs.
